blocks.content: It took about six steps for the table to notice him coming. The conversations didn't stop but they slowed — recalibrating. Who's this. How does he fit.
Jonathan looked up and nodded at the open seat.
Larry sat. A guy across the table — varsity jacket, the kind of big that didn't stop growing — glanced at Jonathan, then at Larry.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's my boy Larry. He good.
Not 'he's my friend.' Not 'we go way back.' He good. Like clearing someone through a door he didn't open.
Larry ate his lunch. He talked when someone talked to him. He laughed when it seemed like the right time to laugh. He did everything right.
And the whole time a small, quiet part of him was keeping score.
